blocks.content: Chapter 322: Fleeting Rift (4)
As they ascended to the top floor, Ordan's mouth did not stop moving.
Although most of what he said was trivial, Kangnan listened without showing annoyance.

blocks.speaker: Ordan
blocks.content: What percentage of Thormia's population are graduates from magic schools?

blocks.speaker: Kangnan
blocks.content: I believe it's about 72 percent.

blocks.speaker: Ordan
blocks.content: Ah, similar to Yakma then. That's a good sign. School graduates are reliable. Those who come from the streets know only one thing, their adaptability is low, what good would hiring such people do?
Kangnan was displeased when someone unrelated to magic commented so freely on the association's matters.

blocks.speaker: Kangnan
blocks.content: But perhaps improvisation in real-world situations could be considered higher, couldn't it?

blocks.speaker: Ordan
blocks.content: Ha! Improvisation? That's just pettiness.

blocks.speaker: Kangnan
blocks.content: Dirty bureaucracy.
Kangnan's lips twisted.

blocks.speaker: Kangnan
blocks.content: She too wasn't a school graduate. Indeed, how could one who only sees the numbers on paperwork understand reality?
Upon entering the reception room, Kangnan sighed as she looked around.
Even though a foreign diplomat had arrived, someone who was supposed to be on the same floor hadn't yet appeared.

blocks.speaker: Kangnan
blocks.content: Please wait here. I will bring the association president.
As if it were the predetermined course of action, Kangnan diffused the situation and exited the reception room.
Her pace quickened from that moment.
The sound of her high heels, full of killing intent, stopped abruptly.
She turned precisely at a right angle and knocked on the door of the president's office.

blocks.speaker: Kangnan
blocks.content: This is Kangnan. I'm entering.
No response came, not that she expected any.
Kangnan forcefully turned the doorknob and entered.
The room, filled with stifling heat and the smell of sweat, assailed her senses.
The sight of the man sitting on the sofa, his groin indecently accentuated by tight square-cut briefs and his legs shamelessly spread,
Offended her.
The man, a magician who should abstain from tobacco, was clenching a particularly potent cigar between his teeth while gazing at the ceiling.
He looked as if he had just finished exercising, his chest heaving.
His wide chest and lean waist, uncharacteristic of a typical magician, his thighs as solid as rock, and his calves sharply defined, did not fit the mold.
However, if any woman other than Kangnan saw the man's body, they might scream. It was covered in scars, looking as if hundreds of worms had writhed over him.

blocks.speaker: Man
blocks.content: What do you want, mongrel?
Kangnan's expression grew fierce.
She had met this man when she was wandering the world alone after the annihilation of the Wolf Tribe and had started a new life with him, but now he seemed not a benefactor but a sworn enemy.

blocks.speaker: Kangnan
blocks.content: What do you mean what do I want? I told you that an urgent emissary from Yakma has arrived. They are waiting in the reception room; what are you doing here?
The man's head slowly rose from behind the sofa.
Despite his muscular body, his face was rugged, with pronounced cheekbones.
A scar ran vertically over his left eye, and his lower jaw was also covered in scars, indicating his long sufferings.
Micae Gaold, the president of the Thormia Magic Association.
He was a first-class grand magician of the Red Line and a founding member of the Alpheus Magic School's Supernatural Psychics Research Society.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: An emissary... came?

blocks.speaker: Kangnan
blocks.content: Don't you remember? I told you just an hour ago! Go and receive them now.
Gaold seemed lost in thought.
He couldn't remember. Lately, it seemed he was forgetting more frequently.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: What did it matter?
He deeply inhaled his cigar and said curtly,

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: No.
Kangnan closed her eyes.
She had to be patient.
Thinking of it as saving a pitiful wretch,
She reopened her eyes and pulled out an official document sealed with the royal emblem.

blocks.speaker: Kangnan
blocks.content: His Highness Adolf himself issued this order. The matter is serious and requires your cooperation regardless of the situation. If you still refuse, I will proceed according to the law.
Gaold leaned back on the sofa, unconcerned, continuing to puff on his cigar with annoyance.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: It's damn troublesome. Can't even be left alone for a second.

blocks.speaker: Kangnan
blocks.content: Receiving an emissary is a matter of courtesy every second. Please stop acting like a spoiled brat.
After finishing her sentence, Kangnan muttered under her breath,

blocks.speaker: Kangnan
blocks.content: Like a duckling…
Gaold lifted his head.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: What did you say?

blocks.speaker: Kangnan
blocks.content: …to stop acting like one.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: That's what I said earlier!

blocks.speaker: Kangnan
blocks.content: I was emphasizing.
Kangnan adjusted her glasses with a prim expression and walked towards the wardrobe.
Reluctantly, Gaold got up as she pulled out his official robes.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Damn! Just let it be something trivial, or I'll show you.
He roughly wiped off the sweat and changed his clothes.
Throwing his cigar on the floor, Gaold strode out of the room and headed toward the reception room.
Opening the door, Ordan greeted him brightly.
He couldn't treat a secretary and the president of the association the same way.
After the pleasantries, Ordan got straight to the point.
Even as the top emissary, his eyes changed once business began.
An aide brought a portable video recording device to the table.
Then, a small safe arrived, and Ordan unlocked it himself.
Inside the safe was Obscura B.

blocks.speaker: Ordan
blocks.content: Currently, anomalies are occurring around the world including in Yakma. Let's watch the video first.
Once Obscura B was connected, a video appeared.
It showed a provincial city still retaining ancient features.
Gaold, who had been watching indifferently, suddenly showed interest as his eyes lit up.
People with tongues multiplying inside their mouths were seen eating others.
Screams
The guard was dispatched.
However, the situation escalated uncontrollably as the guards bitten by the mutants underwent the same mutations.

blocks.speaker: Ordan
blocks.content: This happened in a region called Kubelin. Besides Yakma, we haven't obtained videos from other countries. However, rumors say similar diseases have spread. In some areas, there are reports of ghosts.
Gaold focused solely on the video.
Eventually, the kingdom's special forces intervened and subdued the mutants.
Some of the magicians collecting samples were ones Gaold knew by name.
Magicians collected samples and set the entire village ablaze with a massive flame.

blocks.speaker: Ordan
blocks.content: After the incident, we performed autopsies on the deceased. Any kind of mutation is based on existing properties. However, the mutations in the deceased did not match the characteristics of any known creature here.
Gaold returned to his indifferent attitude and yawned lazily.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: So what, are you saying this came from space?
The video Ordan showed must be top-secret information from Yakma.
Showing it to a foreign magician and sharing key information meant they expected something in return.
Ordan, Yakma's top diplomat, couldn't have missed Gaold's reaction.
But today wasn't about setting up a negotiation table. It was a transnational matter, with the world's eyes on this meeting.

blocks.speaker: Ordan
blocks.content: It seems something has gone wrong with Miro's Space-Time.
Gaold, who seemed weary, suddenly showed a spark of interest in his eyes, but it was a fleeting change, and his face soon returned to its usual calm.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: So what? What do you want me to do?

blocks.speaker: Ordan
blocks.content: We'd like to investigate Miro's Space-Time. As you know, it can only be accessed through the Kingdom of Thormia... Of course, I'm not asking for it to be independently opened. The Valkyries have already approved it.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: That would be through the Alpheus Magic School, precisely.
Gaold showed his displeasure.
Even if he had become a reclusive amnesiac, there were memories he could never forget. Vivid memories of reality still torturously whipped his mind and body.
Gaold leaned back and crossed his legs.
Manners long forgotten from his mind.
Kangnan sighed.
But he didn't care.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: I'll think about it. You can go back now.
Faced with an unexpected response, Ordan's expression turned incredulous.

blocks.speaker: Ordan
blocks.content: Think about it? Do you think I came here to ask you for a favor? This is about international cooperation. I understand His Highness Adolf has already agreed...
Gaold's facial muscles twisted abnormally.
As if a demon's soul had possessed Gaold's face, two extreme expressions appeared simultaneously.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Do you think mentioning the king will make me back down? Adolf? Who the hell is he? No one goes without my permission.
The air heated up, and the room's furnishings began to tremble.
The emotional conflict between wanting to kill Ordan and restraining himself moved the objects.
This was an extreme beyond human limits. Even Kangnan couldn't intervene this time and swallowed hard.
Ordan hallucinated.
The reception room disappeared, and only he and Gaold sat facing each other, surrounded by hellish flames.
The smell of sulfur was intense.
Screams of the damned

blocks.speaker: Ordan
blocks.content: What kind of life had this man lived? Having met countless harsh people in the world, this was the first time someone had truly terrified him.

blocks.speaker: Ordan
blocks.content: They say the president of the Thormia Magic Association is a madman…
Kangnan understood why she hadn't been expelled from the association despite beating many nobles. The head of the association himself was an unparalleled lunatic.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Get out. Before I kill you.
A man who referred to his own king in such terms. Gaold's words made Ordan realize there was not an iota of falsehood in them.
Screaming
He fled the reception room as urine ran down his trousers.
The incident had already occurred, so the reception room was enveloped in heavy silence.
As Gaold's killing intent subsided, Kangnan sat in Ordan's place.

blocks.speaker: Kangnan
blocks.content: Did it really have to go this far? You could have sent him away nicely.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: The bastard deserved it. You enjoyed it too, didn't you?
Kangnan couldn't deny it.
Gaold pulled out a cigar from his pocket and flicked his fingers, igniting a flame that lit the cigar.
In the association where magic controls were active, Gaold was the only one who could wield magic.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: You want me to investigate Miro's Space-Time? Might as well stick my neck out and ask you to smash the back of my head, right?

blocks.speaker: Kangnan
blocks.content: But what really happened? If there's an issue with Miro's Space-Time, Thormia should be the first to show signs.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Probably. It just hasn't materialized yet. You said the tongue monsters appeared in Kubelin, right? Where the ancient Colosseum was.

blocks.speaker: Kangnan
blocks.content: What does that mean?

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: It's not a creature. More like... a concept, I suppose. That concept has crossed over here and settled in areas where human thoughts are concentrated, maybe something like that?
Kangnan nodded compliantly.
If Gaold said so, it must be true. No one in Valkyrie's command knew as much about the Labyrinth and heaven as Gaold did.

blocks.speaker: Kangnan
blocks.content: Why did such phenomena occur?

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: The creatures couldn't cross over. The most likely assumption is that Miro's Space-Time has suffered significant cracks. The Labyrinth could repair that extent. But it won't hold out for long.
Gaold leaned back, fitting the cigar between his fingers. The acrid smoke from the tobacco filled the reception room.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Kangnan, you'll have to go.
Kangnan straightened up, placing her hands on her knees.

blocks.speaker: Kangnan
blocks.content: Give your command.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Something similar will happen here soon. Investigate everything. Start with places where ancient thoughts might congregate—historical sites, Colosseums, old church grounds.
Kangnan noted Gaold's orders in her notebook and sprang to her feet, heading for the door.
If it was a solo mission, she wouldn't need a carriage, so no matter where an incident occurred in the kingdom, she wouldn't be late.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Ah, and while you're at it, swing by Creas too.
As Kangnan turned to leave, she paused and looked back.

blocks.speaker: Kangnan
blocks.content: The place with the magic school? Do you have a message for Alpheus?
Gaold tapped on the sofa.
He was lost in thought.
It might seem premature, but if there was an issue with Miro's Space-Time, there was no time to waste. It was time to enact a plan he had devoted his life to.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Meet a kid named Shirone.
